sections.paragraph:
χλεύη, ης, ἡ (s. χλευάζω; the pl. as early as Hom. Hymns; the sing. in Dio Chrys. 14 [31], 31; Lucian et al.; POxy 904, 2 [V a.d.]; Sb 5763, 51; TestSol 4:11; TestJob 32:11; SibOr 4, 37; Philo; Jos., Bell. 4, 157, Ant. 7, 61; Tat. 30, 2) scorn, ridicule Dg 4:4.—DELG. M-M s.v. χλευάζω.

  1. pl. = plural

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

sing. sing. = singular

Dio Chrys Dio Chrys , I–II a.d.—List 5

Lucian Lucian , II a.d.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

POxy POxy = Oxyrhynchus Papyri—List 4

Sb Sb = Sammelbuch griechischer Urkunden aus Aegypten—Lists 3, 4

TestSol TestSol = Testament of Solomon, I–III a.d.—List 2

TestJob TestJob = Testament of Job—List 2

SibOr SibOr = Oracula Sibyllina, II–III a.d.—List 5

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Tat Tat , II a.d.—List 5

Dg Dg = Letter of Diognetus, attributed to an unidentifiable apologist; II a.d.—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 1085
